titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Barbara Hammer Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis and Timothy G. Young

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 24 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 1,376 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday June 20, 2023 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Barbara Hammer Papers, which span from 1920 to 2021, contain writings, research materials, and documentation related to Hammer's films and artwork including Nitrate Kisses , Resisting Paradise , and Lover Other . The papers also contain correspondence, clippings, ephemera, audiovisual materials, and electronic media related to her life and work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 46-66, 108-118 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 67-68, 121-122 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 120 (record album storage): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Barbara Hammer, on the Edwin J. Beinecke Fund, 2017 and 2018.
September 2019 Acquisition: Gift of A. L. Steiner.
November 2021 Acquisition: Gift of the Estate of Barbara Hammer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: I. August 2017 Acquisition, 1940-2017. II. September 2018 Acquisition, 1920-2018. III. September 2019 Acquisition, 2018. IV. November 2021 Acquisition, 1957-2021.

bioghist.head: Barbara Hammer
bioghist.p: Barbara Hammer (1939-2019) was a filmmaker, visual artist, and teacher of filmmaking and visual arts. Born in Hollywood, California, Hammer created over eighty films and videos during her career, including her most well-known work, Nitrate Kisses. Hammer's film, photography, and artwork was featured in international film festivals, museum retrospectives, and exhibitions all over the world. Hammer also taught filmmaking and visual arts at institutions including the European Graduate School in Saas-Fee, Switzerland.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Barbara Hammer Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Barbara Hammer Papers, which span from 1920 to 2021, contain writings, images, research materials, project files, and documentation related to Hammer's films and artwork. The papers also contain personal and professional correspondence, clippings, ephemera, costumes, audiovisual materials, and electronic media related to her life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Barbara Hammer Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into six series: I. Writings and personal papers, 1940-2017. II. Film projects, artwork, and research, 1980-2017. III. Teaching Notes, 1970-2010. IV. Audiovisual materials, circa 1974-2017. V. Electronica media, 1997-2014. VI. Costumes, clothing, and accessories, 1979-2015.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 46-55 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs and ephemera belonging to Barbara Hammer's father John Hammer, items from the time of Hammer's marriage to Clayton Ward, and materials related to trips and events with family and friends.

did.unittitle: Project photographs, film stills, and related materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and materials from films, events, and projects including Nitrate Kisses, Melbourne Queer Film Festival, Out of South Africa, History Lessons, Doll House, The Great Goddess, The Female Project, Psychosynthesis, The Great Golden, Dream Age, Vital Signs, My Babushka, Double Strength, Multiple Orgasm, 8-in-8 installation, and others.

did.unittitle: Materials exhibited in "BARBARA HAMMER: Evidentiary Bodies" exhibition at Leslie-Lohman Museum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials related to "Camargo," "Woodstock," "Gypsys," "Stephanie Liskey," "Radcliffe Institute," projects, along with unlabeled photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains posters for festivals and events, along with awards and other materials related to Hammer's films and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters for Lover Other, Stonewall Center film series, SUNY Buffalo film series, Both Sides of the Camera, and "Women in film, screening and discussion of Resisting Paradise."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: I. Work and personal files, 1970-2018. II. Posters, art, photographs and awards, 1920-2018. III. Costumes, clothing and accessories, 1970-2018. IV. Media and related materials, 1940-2018.

did.unittitle: Annotated text for "The Art of Dying (Palliative Art Making in an Age of Anxiety" presentation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains annotated script for Barbara Hammer's presentation at Yale University on September 11, 2018.
